description_long: This is a pair of vintage metal pliers with a dark, weathered finish, likely made from forged steel. The handles are long, slightly curved, and show signs of wear, suggesting frequent use. The jaws are broad and flat, designed for cutting or gripping, with a visible pivot joint secured by a central rivet. The surface of the handles appears smooth, lacking any rubberized or ergonomic grip, and the overall construction is robust and utilitarian. There are no visible brand markings or logos on the tool. The pliers have a utilitarian, industrial look, suitable for heavy-duty tasks in urban maintenance or repair settings.
description: A pair of vintage, dark metal pliers with broad flat jaws and long, slightly curved handles. The surface is smooth and worn, with no visible branding or grip pattern.
description_view_0: The pliers are seen edge-on, highlighting the thin profile and the alignment of the handles and jaws.
description_view_1: A clear side view showing the full length of the handles and the broad, flat jaws, as well as the central pivot rivet.
description_view_2: The pliers are viewed from the opposite edge, emphasizing the curvature of the handles and the thickness of the jaws.
description_view_3: Another side view, similar to view 1, but from the opposite side, showing the same features with slight differences in handle curvature.
attribute_handle_type: Long, slightly curved, uninsulated metal handles with a smooth finish.
attribute_jaw_design: Broad, flat jaws with a straight cutting edge, lacking serrations or notches, suitable for general gripping and cutting.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed central rivet pivot, non-adjustable, allowing standard opening and closing motion.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth metal surface with no visible grip pattern or texturing.
